# UC-14.3.4 · Sound Level Anomalies

## Description

Unusual sound patterns near equipment indicate mechanical issues. Edge Hub's stereo microphone enables acoustic monitoring without external sensors.

## Value

Unusual sound patterns near equipment indicate mechanical issues. Edge Hub's stereo microphone enables acoustic monitoring without external sensors.

## Implementation

Deploy Edge Hub near critical equipment. The built-in stereo microphone captures ambient sound levels. Enable kNN anomaly detection to baseline normal patterns and detect deviations. Alert on sustained high levels (OSHA >85dB threshold) and sudden changes (potential equipment failure). Sound data streams as metrics to `edge-hub-data`; anomalies to `edge-hub-anomalies`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Edge Hub (built-in stereo microphone), Splunk OT Intelligence.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=edge-hub-data` (metrics index), `sourcetype=edge_hub` — built-in stereo microphone.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Deploy Edge Hub near critical equipment. The built-in stereo microphone captures ambient sound levels. Enable kNN anomaly detection to baseline normal patterns and detect deviations. Alert on sustained high levels (OSHA >85dB threshold) and sudden changes (potential equipment failure). Sound data streams as metrics to `edge-hub-data`; anomalies to `edge-hub-anomalies`.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| mstats count where index=edge-hub-anomalies AND metric_name=sound_level AND type="anomaly-detector"
  span=1h by extracted_host
| where count > 0
```

Understanding this SPL

**Sound Level Anomalies** — Unusual sound patterns near equipment indicate mechanical issues. Edge Hub's stereo microphone enables acoustic monitoring without external sensors.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=edge-hub-data` (metrics index), `sourcetype=edge_hub` — built-in stereo microphone. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Edge Hub (built-in stereo microphone), Splunk OT Intelligence. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: edge-hub-anomalies.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `mstats` to query metrics indexes (pre-aggregated metric data).
• Filters the current rows with `where count > 0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Note: for mstats, confirm the metrics indexes in the search exist and your role can run mstats. This is separate from CIM data model acceleration.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm metric or event volume in the time range, then compare trends and device status to the Splunk Edge Hub admin UI or the mobile app for the same devices. Verify metric names, hosts, and time skew.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (sound level trend), Single value (current dB), Timeline (anomaly events).

## SPL

```spl
| mstats count where index=edge-hub-anomalies AND metric_name=sound_level AND type="anomaly-detector"
  span=1h by extracted_host
| where count > 0
```

## Visualization

Line chart (sound level trend), Single value (current dB), Timeline (anomaly events).
